summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a high‑energy Pirates World show with a soaring Dark Star, blistering Good Lovin' and a fiery Don't Ease Me In.
review: The recording is an excellent SBD mix that’s surprisingly clear for 1970, with only a brief splice and a truncated Morning Dew intro that don’t detract from the listening experience. Musically the band is on fire – Dark Star erupts into cosmic realms, Good Lovin' rides a wild groove, and Don't Ease Me In crackles with raw excitement, all backed by tight transitions and inspired playing.

ai_rating.rationale: Overwhelmingly positive reviews (mostly 5‑star) praise both the superb soundboard quality and the exceptional performance; the few minor issues (splice, short tracks) are outweighed by the show’s energy and standout jams.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, well‑balanced SBD with minimal splice; occasional cut of Morning Dew intro but overall clear, low hiss and good audience bleed adds atmosphere.

standout_songs: High Time > Dark Star > The Other One
show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with tight transitions – Good Lovin' into Me and My Uncle, Lovelight into the massive Dark Star/Other One sequence, and a concise yet inspired closing that keeps the momentum high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for scorching leads on Dark Star and Good Lovin’, emotive vocals on Cold Rain/High Time, and dueling guitar work with Bob.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for deep, melodic bass that underpins the jams, especially during the expansive Dark Star where he weaves around Jerry’s lines.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for strong rhythm guitar and a thrilling duel with Jerry in the Morning Dew jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ and vocal command are repeatedly praised throughout Lovelight and other jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Dual drummers described as tight, machine‑gun‑like power; drum solo deemed appropriate and energetic.
